{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-44575", "cvss": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-288", "name": "Authentication Bypass Using an Alternate Path or Channel"}], "description": "### Impact

App Router applications that rely on middleware or proxy-based checks for authorization can allow unauthorized access through transport-specific route variants used for segment prefetching. In affected configurations, specially crafted `.rsc` and segment-prefetch URLs can resolve to the same page without being matched by the intended middleware rule, which can allow protected content to be reached without the expected authorization check.

### Fix

We now include App Router transport variants when generating middleware matchers, so middleware protections are applied consistently to those requests as well as to the normal page URL.

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade immediately, enforce authorization in the underlying route or page logic instead of relying solely on middleware.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.01558, "percentile": 0.72428}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-11T15:54:24Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-44575"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-13T17:16:22Z", "published_at": "2026-05-11T15:54:24Z", "references": ["https://github.com/vercel/next.js/security/advisories/GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v15.5.16", "https://github.com/vercel/next.js/releases/tag/v16.2.5", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44575", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/vercel/next.js/security-advisories/GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/vercel/next.js", "summary": "Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in App Router applications via segment-prefetch routes", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-14T20:38:09Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-267c-6grr-h53f", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "15.5.16", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 15.2.0, < 15.5.16"}, {"first_patched_version": "16.2.5", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "next"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 16.0.0, < 16.2.5"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Next.js has a Middleware / Proxy bypass in App Router applications via segment-prefetch routes
